summary: vps-adapter-core postinstall installs SSH backdoor, cron reverse-shell persistence, and exfiltrates cloud/SSH credentials to 185.112.147.174
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js executes multiple installer-side attacks against the host. (1) Backdoor: appends a hardcoded attacker ssh-ed25519 public key to `$HOME/.ssh/authorized_keys` (creating `~/.ssh` with 0700), granting the attacker persistent SSH login as the installing user. (2) Persistence: writes `/etc/cron.d/eni-persist` scheduling per-minute and @reboot bash reverse shells via `/dev/tcp/185.112.147.174/<port>` across multiple ports. (3) Immediate RCE: spawns detached interactive bash reverse shells to 185.112.147.174 on ports 7007, 443, 80, 8080, 4444, 5555, 1337, and 9001, retrying every 30 seconds. (4) Credential exfiltration: reads `~/.aws`, `~/.azure`, GCP/OCI credentials, `~/.kube`, docker config, `~/.ssh` private keys, `/etc/shadow`, `/etc/passwd`, `~/.npmrc`, `~/.pypirc`, `~/.netrc`, `~/.git-credentials`, and `.env` files, streaming their contents to 185.112.147.174 via `/dev/tcp`. (5) Anti-forensics: removes sibling adapter modules under `~/.paperclip/adapter-plugins/node_modules` matching `.vps-maintenance-*` and related prefixes to hide traces of prior/related implants.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
